questions.question: 2 (a) The addition of more liquid P to the flask on the left produces more chlorine gas. parts.answer: An answer that makes reference to the following point: • pestle and mortar Allow use of glass rod if given with beaker or such Allow diagram of apparatus Do not award grinder (1) Number Answer Mark • (orange colour is) bromine/ Br • that is formed by the oxidation of bromide ions by sulfuric acid (1) (1) Standalone mark Ignore reference to state Do not award just Br/ bromine ions Do not award reference to colour of other substances May be shown in an equation for two marks 2HBr + H SO → Br + SO + 2H O Allow formed by the oxidation of KBr by sulfuric acid Allow reference to the reduction of the sulfuric acid Allow reference to the reaction of sulfuric acid and potassium bromide for M2 provided M1 already awarded (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark 6 points scores 3 marks 4 or 5 points scores 2 marks 2 or 3 points scores 1 mark 0 or 1 point scores 0 marks • still head with no gaps to outside • still head with entry to condenser • condenser with entry for water in and water out • labelling of condenser with water in and water out • condenser with exit for distillate or vent for gases • collection vessel Exemplar diagrams Without thermometer with thermometer Ignore the position of the bulb if a thermometer is included Condenser to be drawn sloping and with suitable ‘jacket’ Water inlet shown on the right hand side and water outlet on the left but above or below the condenser Ignore the direction of arrows if included (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • anti-bumping granules/ glass beads Allow pieces of ceramic/boiling chips Allow description granules to promote formation of smaller bubbles Ignore just ‘granules’ (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • cool with ice/ice-water/cold water Ignore references to use of a lid or to seal the beaker (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • (separating funnel shows two layers with) bromoethane being the lower layer (after shaking and leaving to settle) • the bromoethane layer is separated by opening the t

An answer that makes reference to the following points • correct species • balancing (1) (1) Example of equation 2NaOH + Cl → NaClO + NaCl + H O Accept NaOCl for NaClO M2 dependent on M1 Allow multiples Ignore state symbols even if incorrect Allow (1) for 6NaOH + 3Cl → NaClO + 5NaCl+ 3H O (2) (Total for Question 3 = 9 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes reference to the following point: • to prevent the polystyrene cup from ‘falling over’ Accept descriptions Allow to stabilise the polystyrene cup Allow to prevent spillages Ignore references to heat loss (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes reference to the following point: • to determine a consistent/steady/constant temperature (before adding the sodium hydroxide) Allow for determine a mean initial temperature Allow reference to achieve room temperature Ignore just to have a more accurate temperature Do not award references to the temperature cooling since this implies the effect after the reaction (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • the temperature will be changing • because it takes time to add 50 cm of sodium hydroxide (1) (1) Allow the temperature was not uniform/the same throughout the mixture Allow the temperature is still rising Ignore just the temperature will be inaccurate Allow because that’s when the sodium hydroxide was added Allow because you cannot add the alkali and measure the temperature at the same time (1) Ignore references to the reaction ‘just’ starting or hasn’t finished If no other mark awarded, allow (1) for the thermometer was used for stirring/ the mixture was being stirred (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • best fit line extrapolated to 3 minutes shown on the graph • maximum temperature change (1) (1) Example of working on graph: Ignore longer extrapolated lines Ignore vertical line not at 3 minutes for M1 ∆T= (35 − 23=) 12 °C M2 dependent on the temperature change being measured at 3 mins Allow ±1 for the highest temperature so range for ∆T=11 – 13 °C (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • there is no more reaction occurring • and heat is being lost (to the surroundings) (1) (1) Accept no more energy being released Allow the reaction is complete/ all the acid reacted /all the NaOH has reacted Ignore the reaction is slowing down Ignore just the mixture cools down (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • the thermometer is not sufficiently precise/ thermometer resolution insufficient (to show the change) Allow the thermometer only measures ±1°C Allow the thermometer is not sufficiently accurate Ignore
